brief: The highest-value opportunities are heavily concentrated in Brooklyn R6 and R7 infill lots where a 485-x tax exemption combined with Section 8 VPS rents generates strong cash-on-cash returns reaching up to 31.8%. While Brooklyn offers high-yield market-tier plays with extensive land headroom, Bronx parcels present structured fee-driven options via the ELLA/4% LIHTC program stack. All top-scoring opportunities in this screen are city-owned properties requiring public disposition processes.

targets.play: Develop a 10-unit residential project in R7-2 zoning utilizing 485-x tax incentives and Section 8 VPS rents to achieve a 31.8% cash-on-cash return.
targets.negotiation: Estimated land cost is $569,141 against a walk-away land limit of $3,202,693, providing $2,633,552 in valuation headroom.
targets.risk: Navigating the public HPD/EDC disposition process for city-owned land while confirming site conditions and R7-2 zoning parameters.

targets.play: Construct a 14-unit residential project in an R7A zone leveraging 485-x and Section 8 VPS rents for an 18.3% cash-on-cash return.
targets.negotiation: Estimated land cost is $690,612 versus a walk-away threshold of $2,337,060, leaving $1,646,448 in headroom.
targets.risk: City-owned lot requiring HPD disposition approval alongside physical site condition and environmental due diligence.

targets.play: Build 12 units on R7A zoned land executing the 485-x and Section 8 VPS rent strategy to generate a 16.9% cash-on-cash return.
targets.negotiation: Estimated land cost sits at $591,738 compared to a walk-away land value of $1,850,943, creating $1,259,205 in headroom.
targets.risk: HPD disposition process timelines and zoning verification for the R7A footprint.

targets.play: Execute a 10-unit Bronx affordable play using the ELLA / 4% LIHTC stack to secure a $333,039 developer fee.
targets.negotiation: Estimated land cost is $591,182 against a $57,379 walk-away land price, requiring a nominal or subsidized land transfer to pencil.
targets.risk: High competition for municipal subsidy awards and municipal approval via HPD disposition.

process_note: Because these screening targets are city-owned sites, our immediate next step is monitoring HPD/EDC RFP disposition schedules while running preliminary zoning verification and site condition checks.
disclaimer: AI interpretation of the engine's real screening output. Estimates pending diligence — not investment advice.
